sections.paragraph:
εὐσχημονέω (s. three next entries; Pla., Leg. 732c; Menand.; Philod.; PSI V 541, 5; not LXX; Joseph.) gener. ‘behave with dignity/decorum’: in our lit. only as rdg. of P46 in 1 Cor 13:5 for ἀσχημονεῖ. If it is not a scribal error here, it must have the sense be pretentious, be pompous as outward display of goodness (s. εὐσχήμων 2), which is not found elsewh., but cp. εὐσχημών ‘specious’ Eur., Med. 584. S. ADebrunner, ConNeot XI, ’47, 37–41.—DELG s.v. 1 ἔχω 3.

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

Menand Menand , various dramas cited; IV–III b.c.—List 5

Philod Philod , I b.c.—List 5

PSI PSI = Papiri greci e latini: Pubblicazioni della Società Italiana—List 4

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

gener. gener. = generally

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

rdg. rdg. = reading(s)

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Eur Eur , V b.c.—List 5

ConNeot ConNeot = Coniectanea Neotestamentica—List 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 413
